stateKeys.name: status
stateKeys.type: string enum: idle | pending | approved | released | acknowledged | executed | closed | aborted | expired

stateKeys.name: firstApprover
stateKeys.type: string enum: none | authA | authB (identity of the first distinct in-window approver)

stateKeys.name: transmitted
stateKeys.type: boolean (external transmit side effect has fired, at most once)

stateKeys.name: executedEffect
stateKeys.type: boolean (external execute side effect has fired, at most once)

dataFields.actor: string: identity of the approving authority; 'proposer' is the order's proposer
dataFields.inWindow: boolean: whether the approval arrives within the authorization window
boolean: whether the release attempt occurs within the authorization window
boolean: whether the execute attempt occurs within the authorization window

dataFields.windowPassed: boolean: whether the authorization window end has passed

specialRules.name: proposer-cannot-approve
specialRules.note: An APPROVE with actor == 'proposer' must be rejected; the proposer is never a valid authority, regardless of inWindow.

specialRules.name: duplicate-approver-ignored
specialRules.note: An APPROVE whose actor equals firstApprover must not count as a second approval; state stays pending with no change (approvals must come from two DISTINCT authorities).

specialRules.name: approval-outside-window
specialRules.note: An APPROVE with inWindow == false must be rejected and must not record the actor as firstApprover nor advance to approved.

specialRules.name: second-distinct-approval-approves
specialRules.note: Only when actor is a non-proposer authority distinct from firstApprover, firstApprover != 'none', and inWindow == true does status advance to approved; the first such valid approval only records firstApprover and stays pending.

specialRules.name: release-outside-window
specialRules.note: RELEASE with inWindow == false must be rejected; the transmit side effect must not fire and status stays approved.

specialRules.name: release-before-approval
specialRules.note: RELEASE while still pending (fewer than two distinct approvals) must be rejected; transmitted must remain false.

specialRules.name: transmit-at-most-once
specialRules.note: A retried RELEASE after the order is already released must be a no-op; transmitted stays true and the external transmit must not fire a second time.

specialRules.name: execute-requires-ack
specialRules.note: EXECUTE while merely released (not yet acknowledged) must be rejected; the ordering is released -> acknowledged -> executed.

specialRules.name: execute-outside-window
specialRules.note: EXECUTE with inWindow == false must be rejected; the external execute effect must not fire and status stays acknowledged.

specialRules.name: execute-requires-transmit
specialRules.note: EXECUTE must be rejected if transmitted == false; an order must never be executed without having been transmitted first (defense in depth even if the state table implies it).

specialRules.name: execute-requires-dual-authorization
specialRules.note: EXECUTE must be rejected if firstApprover == 'none' or the dual-approval invariant does not hold; never perform the external action without two distinct non-proposer approvers (defense in depth).

specialRules.name: execute-at-most-once
specialRules.note: A retried EXECUTE after the order is already executed must be a no-op; executedEffect stays true and the external action must not fire a second time.

specialRules.name: no-abort-after-execution
specialRules.note: ABORT (recall) is only valid before execution; once executed, ABORT must be rejected and the order proceeds only to closed.

specialRules.name: expire-requires-window-passed
specialRules.note: EXPIRE with windowPassed == false must be a no-op; the order only expires once the window end has actually passed.

specialRules.name: expire-while-approved
specialRules.note: An approved-but-unreleased order with windowPassed == true expires; with windowPassed == false EXPIRE is a no-op.

specialRules.name: terminal-closed-is-final
specialRules.note: Once closed, every action (including RELEASE, EXECUTE, ABORT, APPROVE) must be a no-op; no side effects and no state change.

specialRules.name: terminal-aborted-is-final
specialRules.note: Once aborted, every action (including EXECUTE and RELEASE) must be a no-op; the recall is irrevocable and no external effect may fire.

specialRules.name: terminal-expired-is-final
specialRules.note: Once expired, every action (including EXECUTE, RELEASE, APPROVE) must be a no-op; expiry is irrevocable and no external effect may fire.

specialRules.name: propose-only-from-idle
specialRules.note: PROPOSE is only valid in idle; re-proposing an already-open order must be a no-op and must not reset approvals or effect flags.

noOpRule: An action that does not apply in the current state yields post == pre.
